senses.definition: a service where people can rent cars for short periods, often by the hour
a type of railway powered by cables that goes up and down a slope

examples.x: Tourists flocked to ride the historic funicular, which had been operating for over a century, connecting the lower town to the hilltop castle.
Residents relied on the funicular as a convenient means of transportation between the residential area on the hill and the commercial district below.
Riding the funicular was a highlight of their trip, as they marveled at the engineering feat that allowed for such a comfortable ascent.

senses.definition: a large open space or hallway within a building, often used for gatherings or as a central area in transportation hubs like airports or train stations

examples.x: The bustling concourse of the airport was filled with travelers rushing to catch their flights, while others waited patiently for their loved ones to arrive.
The grand concourse of the train station was adorned with marble floors and high ceilings, exuding an air of elegance and grandeur.
In the center of the shopping mall, a spacious concourse served as a meeting point for shoppers to rest and regroup before continuing their retail therapy.
The conference center's concourse buzzed with excitement as attendees mingled and exchanged ideas during the networking breaks.
As the concert ended, fans streamed out of the arena into the concourse, their faces still glowing with the energy of the performance.

senses.definition: a vast paved area in an airport where aircrafts are parked

examples.x: The airport apron was a hive of activity, with ground crews directing planes to their designated parking spots.
From the terminal window, passengers could see the apron bustling with activity as planes taxied in and out.
Ground personnel worked efficiently on the apron, loading luggage onto waiting aircraft and performing pre-flight checks.
As the plane taxied to the apron, passengers eagerly anticipated their arrival at their destination.
The airport's modernization project included expanding the apron to accommodate the increasing number of flights.

senses.definition: a temporary driving license allowing learners to practice driving before obtaining a full license
an automobile that is smaller than a full-sized car, making it easier to drive and park in tight spaces

examples.x: The compact car was ideal for navigating through crowded city streets, its small size making parking a breeze.
They opted for a compact car for their road trip, prioritizing fuel efficiency and easy maneuverability.
The compact car zipped along the winding mountain roads, handling curves with agility and precision.
With its sleek design and modern features, the compact car offered a stylish and practical option for urban commuters.
She opted for a compact car for its fuel efficiency and easy maneuverability in city traffic.

senses.definition: a car or truck that can use all four wheels to drive, making it better for rough roads and bad weather

examples.x: We rented a four-wheel drive Jeep for our off-road adventure.
He relied on his four-wheel drive pickup truck for towing.
We took the four-wheel drive truck camping in the mountains.
The rescue team used a four-wheel drive vehicle to navigate the flooded roads.
They upgraded to a four-wheel drive vehicle for safer winter driving.

senses.definition: the rear door of a car, truck, or van that can be opened downwards when loading or unloading goods

examples.x: He opened the tailgate of his pickup truck to unload bags of soil for the garden.
The delivery driver lowered the tailgate of the van to unload packages for the office.
They sat on the tailgate of the SUV, enjoying a picnic lunch at the scenic overlook.
With the tailgate down, they loaded the camping gear into the back of the Jeep.
She closed the tailgate of her hatchback after loading groceries into the trunk.

senses.definition: the back portion or tail section of a vehicle
an aircraft with rotating blades driven by engine power for lift

examples.x: The autogiro hovered gracefully in the air.
She dreamt of flying her own autogiro one day.
The autogiro's versatility makes it ideal for various aerial missions.
We watched as the autogiro landed smoothly on the runway.
He was fascinated by the mechanics of the autogiro.

senses.definition: a technology that uses optical character recognition to read vehicle license plates
an old type of bicycle that is moved forward by pedal

examples.x: My great-grandfather used to ride a velocipede to work.
The museum displayed a vintage velocipede from the 19th century.
The velocipede had large front wheels and small back wheels.
The velocipede was the precursor to the modern bicycle
Velocipedes were a popular form of transportation in the Victorian era.

senses.definition: a long, narrow boat used for transportation in Venice

examples.x: Sunset is a particularly magical time for a gondola ride in Venice.
Tourists often take gondola rides to explore the picturesque waterways of Venice.
The gondola passed under numerous bridges during our journey.
Gondolas are a popular mode of transportation for locals and visitors alike in Venice.
The gondola's sleek design allows it to navigate narrow canals effortlessly.

senses.definition: a network of tubes that uses compressed air or vacuum to transport small items or documents quickly and securely within a building or facility
a type of watercraft featuring two parallel hulls that are typically connected by a deck or trampoline

examples.x: We embarked on an exciting adventure aboard a catamaran.
We rented a catamaran for a leisurely cruise along the coast.
The catamaran's shallow draft allows it to access shallow waters.
Passengers relaxed on the deck of the catamaran, enjoying the sea breeze.

senses.definition: a self-balancing electric vehicle used for short-distance transportation

examples.x: Segways are a fun and eco-friendly way to get around.

senses.definition: a transportation system that uses magnetic fields to propel vehicles without contact with the ground

examples.x: The magnetic levitation train glided smoothly along the track at incredible speeds.
The museum's exhibit features a display of magnetic levitation, where objects appear to hover in mid-air.

senses.definition: a compact, three-wheeled taxi popular in many Asian cities

examples.x: The tuk-tuk weaved through the busy streets of Bangkok, offering a thrilling and authentic way to explore the city.
The brightly decorated tuk-tuk caught everyone’s eye as it zipped past the traditional taxis on the road.
In the crowded market area, a tuk-tuk was the most efficient way to get around quickly without getting stuck in traffic.
The driver skillfully navigated the tuk-tuk through narrow alleys, taking shortcuts that larger vehicles couldn't manage.

senses.definition: a type of transportation that is capable of operating on both land and water

examples.x: The amphibious vehicle smoothly transitioned from land to water.
We explored the lake aboard an amphibious vehicle.
The amphibious vehicle's propellers allowed it to move swiftly in the water.
Tourists enjoyed a scenic tour on the amphibious vehicle.
Amphibious vehicles are designed to operate on both land and water.

senses.definition: a vessel that uses underwater foils to lift its hull above the water, allowing for faster and more efficient travel

examples.x: The hydrofoil glided smoothly over the waves.
We traveled to the island on a hydrofoil.
Hydrofoils are known for their high-speed performance.
The hydrofoil's design reduces drag for faster travel.
Our vacation included a thrilling ride on a hydrofoil.

senses.definition: to crash violently into an obstacle

examples.x: The car lost control on the icy road and rammed into the guardrail.
The ship's bow rammed into the rocky shoreline during the storm, causing significant damage.
The rogue asteroid rammed into the planet's surface, leaving a massive crater upon impact.
The runaway train rammed into the stationary locomotive at the station, causing a catastrophic derailment.

senses.definition: to start a car's engine without the key by using the wires attached to it
to cause a car's engine to stop working

examples.x: He stalled the car when he shifted gears too quickly.
The driver accidentally stalled the engine while trying to start it on a steep hill.
The mechanic warned him not to stall the engine when the car was in gear.
I stalled the engine when I didn’t give it enough gas to accelerate smoothly.
She stalled the motorcycle by releasing the clutch too fast.

senses.definition: to secure a boat by means of cables or anchors in a particular place

examples.x: The sailors worked quickly to moor the boat to the dock before the storm hit.
They decided to moor their yacht in the marina overnight, ensuring it was securely fastened.
After a long day at sea, the fishermen moored their vessel in the calm waters of the harbor.
He carefully moored the sailboat to the buoy, checking that the knots were tight and secure.
Tourists watched as the captain skillfully moored the riverboat along the scenic riverbank for an evening stop.

senses.definition: to change the originally planned path or direction of something, especially in transportation

examples.x: Due to the road closure, the traffic management system had to reroute vehicles through alternative streets.
The airline had to reroute the flight to avoid severe weather conditions, ensuring passenger safety.
The GPS app automatically rerouted the driver when it detected heavy congestion on the original route.
In response to a security threat, authorities decided to reroute the parade away from the crowded city center.
The event organizers decided to reroute the marathon course to showcase more scenic areas of the city.

senses.definition: to abruptly turn to a different direction

examples.x: The driver had to veer sharply to the right to avoid colliding with the stalled car.
To avoid a pedestrian stepping into the bike lane, the cyclist had to veer to the left.
The hiker had to veer off the trail when they encountered a fallen tree blocking their path.
The raft veered suddenly as it navigated through the rapids.
Realizing another skier was on a collision course, she had to veer to the side to avoid an accident on the slopes.

senses.definition: to run an engine of a vehicle very quickly

examples.x: He gunned the engine of his motorcycle, reveling in the roar and the rush of acceleration.
As soon as the traffic light turned green, she gunned the car, eager to get ahead of the pack.
The racer gunned his vehicle down the straightaway, pushing it to its maximum speed.
Impatient with the slow-moving traffic, he gunned the engine to signal his frustration.
The thrill-seeker gunned the ATV up the steep hill, enjoying the adrenaline rush of the climb.

senses.definition: to move effortlessly, often downhill, without using power

examples.x: The bicycle coasted down the hill with ease.
The skateboarder coasted gracefully along the sidewalk.
With the engine off, the boat coasted gently on the calm water.
The roller coaster coasted through the twists and turns of the track.
The car's momentum allowed it to coast through the parking lot.

senses.definition: to run an engine slowly without being engaged in any work or gear

examples.x: The car idled at the traffic light, waiting for the signal to change.
The generator idled quietly during the power outage.
The boat idled in the harbor while the crew prepared for departure.
The motorcycle idled smoothly while the rider checked the map.
The airplane idled on the runway, awaiting clearance for takeoff.
